req.title: Manager creates department
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The violation was test[3] having kind=negative but heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) is not a valid heuristic for a simple negative/unauthenticated test; it should use NEG since it exercises a negative authentication state. Fix: change test[3] heuristic from STC to NEG. All other tests are preserved as-is.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_department_valid_payload
preconditions: Department name does not already exist
actions: POST /departments with valid name and description as manager
Query audit log for the returned entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the created department id
trace: R-WEB-005: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_creates_department_valid_payload(
    manager_api_client,       # <fixture>: authenticated httpx client for manager role
    unique_dept_name,          # <fixture>: generates a unique department name string
    audit_log_query,           # <helper_fn>: queries audit log by entity id
    cleanup_department,        # <fixture>: deletes department by id after test
):
    payload = {"name": unique_dept_name, "description": "Engineering unit"}
    response = manager_api_client.post("/departments", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"

    cleanup_department(entity_id)

    audit_records = audit_log_query(entity_id=entity_id, action="CREATE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful creation"

tests.name: test_create_department_missing_mandatory_name_returns_400
actions: POST /departments with payload omitting the mandatory 'name' field
Capture response status and validation error body
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing the 'name' field
trace: R-WEB-005: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_create_department_name_at_maximum_boundary_length
preconditions: Maximum allowed department name length is 100 characters (per data dictionary)
actions: POST /departments with name exactly 100 characters long
Capture response status and entity id
POST /departments with name exactly 101 characters long
Capture response status for over-boundary case
expected: 100-character name: response status is 200 or 201 with entity id
trace: R-WEB-005: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_create_department_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No active session or auth token (logged-out state)
actions: POST /departments with valid payload but without any Authorization header

tests.name: test_create_department_forbidden_role_returns_403
actions: POST /departments with valid payload using an employee-role token
